_comment: WC2026 Group L, MD3. Standings before: England 4 (4-2 Croatia, 0-0 Ghana), Ghana 4 (1-0 Panama, 0-0 England), Croatia 3, Panama 0 (0-1 Ghana, 0-1 Croatia). England the clear quality side chasing top spot; Panama eliminated (0 pts, GD -2, a best third is out of reach) but a DISCIPLINED low block -- they lost both only 0-1, conceding just one a game and parking the bus. So this is the recurring 'favourite vs organized low block' archetype (Ecuador-Curaçao, England-Ghana, Spain-Cape Verde): England a big favourite but do NOT overrate a rout -- the draw/Under is the live tail, expressed via the RESULT not BTTS-No. Eliminated Panama is NOT over-downgraded (South Africa lesson): a broken-but-organized side can still frustrate. Neutral venue, base symmetric -> 'home' is nominal (user listed Panama first). Home = Panama, away = England. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: England (4 pts) win the group with a result; Panama (0 pts) are eliminated but a disciplined low block. England big favourite; the draw/Under is the value tail if Panama frustrate.

home.name: Panama
notes: Eliminated, scored 0 in two games, but organized: lost both only 0-1. attack 0.62 (no over-downgrade -- South Africa lesson).
Nothing to play for on points, but pride + a parked bus -> can frustrate.

away.name: England
notes: Quality side, beat Croatia 4-2; real firepower (Kane/Bellingham). attack 1.45.
Strong defense, kept Ghana to 0. defense 0.82.
Favourite but a proven low block keeps the draw/Under live -> don't overrate the rout.

_postmatch.result: Panama 0-2 England. England win the group (7 pts, GD +4); Panama finish last (0 pts) and out.
_postmatch.verdict: Full process hit. England won by the model's #2 scoreline (0-2, 16.1%), kept the clean sheet (59.5%), and the goals reads all landed: BTTS No (64.9%), Under 2.5 (55.9%). xG England 1.92 -> 2 goals, almost exact. The 'favourite vs organized block' caveat resolved on the benign side: Panama parked the bus but England broke through cleanly without a rout (2-0, right on lambda), so the draw/Under value tail did not fire but was correctly priced as the minority scenario.
_postmatch.lesson: When a favourite faces a disciplined low block, 2-0/2 goals on-lambda is the modal break-through, not a rout — and not a 0-0 either. Pricing the draw/Under as a live-but-minority tail (here ~21% draw, 9% 0-0) was the honest middle: it didn't hit, but it was the correctly-uncertain hedge, and the central 0-1/0-2 call carried the match.
